objective: Stratégie de Momentum sur ARKMUSDC 5m. Indicateurs : MFI + MACD + ROC + ATR. Entrée sur divergence haussière/baissière entre MFI et MACD. Take-profit sur perte de momentum, stop basé sur ATR. Stop-loss = 1.4x ATR, take-profit = 3.1x ATR.

iterations.hypothesis: The convergence of the MFI and MACD momentum indicators can identify turning points in price movements. Bollinger Bands and ATR are used for risk management. Entry is triggered when both MFI and MACD give a buy signal, exit is based on divergence with bollinger band middle line and low strength ADX

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses momentum indicators to identify potential turning points in price movements. MFI and MACD are used for divergence analysis, while ATR is utilized as stop-loss and take-profit levels. The backtest has not yet completed so the performance of this strategy cannot be determined. If results are poor (negative return), I would consider adjusting parameters or changing indicators to improve performance.
[Backup plan] If necessary, alternative approaches could include using different momentum indicators such as RSI or stochastic oscillators, incorporating volume data into the analysis, or considering other technical and fundamental factors in addition to price movements.

iterations.hypothesis: Trend following strategy using ATR and Bollinger bands with RSI for confirmation signal.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses momentum indicators - MACD, ROC, ATR and Bollinger bands to identify potential trade entries on ARKMUSDC 5m. The entry long is when MACD crosses above its signal line AND ROC > 100 AND ATR> Bollinger Band Middle. Exit: Close falls below Bollinger Band Middle or MACD crosses above its signal line with a momentum indicator crossing below zero. The backtest results so far are negative, showing a return of -25.2% for the first iteration. Potential weaknesses include overfitting and reliance on high trading frequency to compensate for losses. If results do not improve, we can try adjusting parameters such as ROC, ATR thresholds or changing the stop-loss and take-profit values.
[Backup plan] If the current strategy does not yield positive returns, we can explore alternative approaches: 1) Experiment with different momentum indicators like RSI, Williams %R, or Fractals. This may help to capture more price action information. 2) We could also try incorporating market conditions and other fundamental factors into our analysis, such as the VIX index or economic data releases. 3) Lastly, we can consider diversifying trades across different assets or trading strategies.
